data.title: OGE Energy prices $345M stock offering
data.description:
OGE Energy (OGE) priced an offering of 8,023,256 shares at $43.00 each, with an underwriter option to buy up to 1,203,488 additional shares.

Morgan Stanley and J.P. Morgan are acting as joint lead bookrunners of the offering and representatives for the underwriters. In connection with the offering, OGE Energy entered into separate forward sale agreements with each of Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC and JPMorgan Chase Bank, National Association, New York Branch.

The company has agreed to issue and sell directly 4,011,628 shares to the underwriters in the offering, and the forward sellers agreed to borrow from third parties and sell to such underwriters 4,011,628 shares in connection with the forward sale agreements.

OGE Energy (OGE) plans to use the proceeds it receives from the sale of the shares offered and sold directly to its general funds to fund capital expenditures, including the Horseshoe Lake generating units 13 and 14 and Ft. Smith to Muskogee Transmission line, and for other general corporate purposes, including to repay or refinance debt.

data.title: Veolia to acquire hazardous waste company Clean Earth for $3B
data.description:
• Veolia Environnement (OTCPK:VEOEY) has entered into an agreement with Enviri (NVRI) to acquire Clean Earth, a U.S.-based hazardous waste company, for an enterprise value of $3B (or ~€2.6bn).
  • The transaction is aimed to close in mid-2026. It offers strong value creation for shareholders with $120M of synergies by year 4 and current EPS accretion from year 2.
  • Enviri shareholders are expected to receive cash consideration of $14.50 - $16.50 per share in the transaction and retain full ownership of Harsco Environmental and Rail through a spin-off of those businesses into a standalone publicly traded company.
  • The deal will double Veolia’s U.S. hazardous waste footprint, allowing it to offer a full range of environmental services on a nationwide basis. Upon the completion, the group’s hazardous waste revenue will reach €5.2B, with EBITDA margin of 17%. Veolia also raised the target for its hazardous waste activities, now aiming EBITDA growth of at least 10% over 2024-27.

data.title: Cathie Wood’s ARK Invest buys Nvidia shares for first time in months
data.description:
• Cathie Wood’s flagship fund ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) bought 93,374 Nvidia (NVDA) shares on Thursday, the fund’s first purchase of the chipmaker since Aug. 4, according to ARK’s daily trade disclosures.
  • The move came after Nvidia (NVDA) reported stronger-than-expected earnings. Nvidia shares initially rose more than 5% following the results before reversing to close down 3.15% at $180.64 on Thursday, as broader markets fell and investors scaled back expectations for a December Federal Reserve rate cut.
  • As of November 20, ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) held 505,395 Nvidia shares, representing its 23rd largest position.

data.title: UK retail sales fall 1.1% in October, defying forecasts and marking first drop since May
data.description:
UK retail sales volumes dropped 1.1% month-on-month in October 2025, defying forecasts of -0.1%, following an upwardly revised 0.7% growth in September. 

This marked the first decline since May, as supermarket sales declined for the second straight month.

data.title: Frontline Non-GAAP EPS of $0.19 misses by $0.05, revenue of $432.65M beats by $163.63M
data.description:
• Frontline press release (FRO): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.19 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $432.65M (-11.8% Y/Y) beats by $163.63M.
  • Achieved average daily spot time charter equivalent earnings ("TCEs")1 for VLCCs, Suezmax tankers and LR2/Aframax tankers in the third quarter of $34,300, $35,100 and $31,400 per day, respectively.
  • Sold its oldest Suezmax tanker, built in 2011, for a net sales price of $36.4 million and delivered the vessel to its new owner in September 2025. After repayment of existing debt, the transaction generated net cash proceeds of approximately $23.7 million in the third quarter of 2025.
  • "We expect the spot TCEs for the full fourth quarter of 2025 to be lower than the spot TCEs currently contracted, due to the impact of ballast days during the fourth quarter of 2025."

data.title: Foxconn and OpenAI collaborate to strengthen U.S. AI supply chain manufacturing
Asia stocks plunge as renewed tech sell-off and AI valuation fears spook markets, Japan pressured after sticky inflation
data.description:
Asia stocks trade in red on Friday following a sharp overnight slide on Wall Street pressured sentiment, driven by a renewed sell-off in heavyweight technology shares, and U.S. jobs data failed to clarify the near-term interest-rate outlook. 

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) fell toward $85,000 on Friday, marking its lowest level since April as a global selloff in technology stocks, driven by renewed concerns over the artificial intelligence sector, spread to risk assets including cryptocurrencies.

Gold prices fell to around $4,060 per ounce on Friday, heading for a modest weekly decline.

Malaysia’s annual inflation eased to 1.3% in October 2025, down from September’s seven-month high and market expectations of 1.5%. Food prices increased the least in over 4 years (1.5% vs 2.1% in September). 

Singapore’s economy expanded 4.2% year-on-year in Q3 2025, down from an upwardly revised 4.7% in Q2. The latest print was above the flash estimate of 2.9%.

Japan (NKY:IND) fell 2.20% to below 48,700 while the Topix Index slipped 0.5% to 3,282 on Friday, reversing the prior session’s gains. The Japanese yen steadied near 157 per dollar on Friday, halting its recent slide, after Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama suggested that intervention was possible to curb excessive volatility and speculative moves.

Japan’s annual inflation rate edged up to 3.0% in October 2025 from 2.9% in September, marking the highest reading since July. Core inflation also came in at 3.0%, matching forecasts and pointing to the highest in three months.

Japan’s trade deficit decreased significantly to JPY 231.8 billion in October 2025 from JPY 499.9 billion in the same month a year earlier, outperforming market estimates of a shortfall of JPY 280 billion. The improvement was largely driven by robust export growth. 

Separate data showed, the S&P Global Japan Manufacturing PMI rose to 48.8 in November 2025, up from 48.2 in October, which had marked the weakest level in 19 months, preliminary estimates showed. The S&P Global Japan Services PMI held at 53.1 in November 2025, unchanged from October’s final reading, indicating a continued expansion in the services sector. 

China (SHCOMP) fell 1.46% to around 3870, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 2.8% to 12,620 on Friday, pushing mainland stocks to multi-week lows as Chinese technology and AI-linked shares joined a global selloff.

Further pressure came from mounting tensions between China and Japan over Taiwan, including Beijing’s plan to suspend imports of Japanese seafood. 

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 1.64% to 25,191 in early Friday trade, extending bearish momentum since last week and hitting an over one-month low amid a broad-based selloff. 

India (SENSEX) fell 0.44% to 85,233 in morning trade on Friday, halting gains from the previous two sessions. The HSBC India Manufacturing PMI fell to 57.4 in November 2025 from October’s 59.2, below forecasts of 59. The HSBC India Services PMI climbed to 59.5 in November 2025, up from October’s five-month low of 58.9, and slightly below the market forecast of 59.7.

India has launched an anti-dumping investigation into imports of Polyester Textured Yarn originating in or exported from China, following an application filed by Reliance Industries Limited and Wellknown Polyester Limited, the Directorate General of Trade Remedies said Thursday, as reported by the Economic Times. 

Australia (AS51) fell 1.59% to below 8,440 in early Friday trade, pulling back to its lowest level in more than five months amid broad sector losses. 

New Zealand’s trade deficit narrowed to NZD 1.5 billion in October 2025 as export growth outpaced imports, down from NZD 1.65 billion in the same month a year earlier.

South Korea’s producer prices rose 1.5% year-on-year in October 2025, marking the highest increase since February and up from 1.2% in September.

In the U.S. on Thursday, all three major indexes ended in red erased gains to close at one-month lows on Thursday amid the outlook of higher interest rates by the Federal Reserve, as markets reassessed risks that high valuations for AI companies are unwarranted.

U.S. stock futures steadied on Friday after a dramatic reversal in the previous session: Dow +0.46%; S&P 500 +0.37%; Nasdaq +0.23%.

Meanwhile, delayed employment data from the BLS, expected to be the final release before the December FOMC meeting, showed stronger than expected job growth in September.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Japan's annual inflation rises to 3.0% in October, core inflation matches forecasts; manufacturing PMI shrinks the least in 3 months
data.description:
Japan’s annual inflation rate edged up to 3.0% in October 2025 from 2.9% in September, marking the highest reading since July. Core inflation also came in at 3.0%, matching forecasts and pointing to the highest in three month.

Monthly, the CPI rose 0.4%, recording the highest level since January.

Food prices in Japan increased by 6.4% year-on-year in October 2025, easing from a 6.7% gain in the previous month and marking the softest rise since December 2024. 

Separate data showed, the S&P Global Japan Manufacturing PMI rose to 48.8 in November 2025, up from 48.2 in October, which had marked the weakest level in 19 months, preliminary estimates showed. The S&P Global Japan Services PMI held at 53.1 in November 2025, unchanged from October’s final reading, indicating a continued expansion in the services sector. The S&P Global Japan Composite PMI edged higher to 52.0 in November 2025 from a final 51.5 in the previous month, marking the highest level since August. 

Japan’s trade deficit decreased significantly to JPY 231.8 billion in October 2025 from JPY 499.9 billion in the same month a year earlier, outperforming market estimates of a shortfall of JPY 280 billion. The improvement was largely driven by robust export growth. Exports rose 3.6% year-on-year to reach a seven-month high of JPY 9,766.3 billion.

The Nikkei 225 Index to below 48,700 while the Topix Index slipped 0.5% to 3,282 on Friday, reversing the prior session’s gains. The Japanese yen steadied near 157 per dollar on Friday, halting its recent slide, after Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama suggested that intervention was possible to curb excessive volatility and speculative moves.

More on Japan

  • Japan's trade deficit shrinks sharply in October, exports hit 7-month high

  • BoJ minutes reveal ongoing caution on economy amid inflation & market risks
  • Japan's manufacturing PMI revised upward to 48.7 in April
  • Japan to spend ¥388 billion on relief measures for U.S. tariff impact
  • Japan services sector expands for sixth month, but jobless rate hits 13-month high

data.title: Japan's trade deficit shrinks sharply in October, exports hit 7-month high
data.description:
Japan’s trade deficit decreased significantly to JPY 231.8 billion in October 2025 from JPY 499.9 billion in the same month a year earlier, outperforming market estimates of a shortfall of JPY 280 billion.

The improvement was largely driven by robust export growth. Exports rose 3.6% year-on-year to reach a seven-month high of JPY 9,766.3 billion. This marked the second consecutive month of growth and significantly outpaced forecasts for a modest 1.1% gain, even though the pace eased slightly from September’s 4.2% increase.

Analysts expect U.S.-bound shipments to stay weak as automakers shift tariff costs to consumers after initially absorbing them through price cuts.

Japan’s imports increased 0.7% year-on-year to a nine-month high of JPY 9,998.1 billion in October 2025, beating market expectations for a 0.7% decline and marking a second straight month of growth, reflecting front-loaded purchases ahead of winter. 

The Nikkei 225 Index to below 48,700 while the Topix Index slipped 0.5% to 3,282 on Friday, reversing the prior session’s gains. The Japanese yen steadied near 157 per dollar on Friday, halting its recent slide, after Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama suggested that intervention was possible to curb excessive volatility and speculative moves.

More on Japan

  • BoJ minutes reveal ongoing caution on economy amid inflation & market risks
  • Japan's manufacturing PMI revised upward to 48.7 in April
  • Japan to spend ¥388 billion on relief measures for U.S. tariff impact
  • Japan services sector expands for sixth month, but jobless rate hits 13-month high

  • Takaichi elected LDP Chief, poised to become Japan’s first female prime minister

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
07:30 AM Fed's Williams Speech

New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams gives keynote before the Annual Conference of the Central Bank of Chile.

08:30 AM Fed's Barr Speech

Federal Reserve Board Governor Michael Barr will give welcome remarks at the College Fed Challenge Finals, Washington, D.C.

09:00 AM Fed's Logan Speech

Dallas Federal Reserve Bank President Lorie Logan participates in moderated panel before the"SNB and its Watchers 2025" event.

09:00 AM Fed's Collins Speech 

Boston Federal Reserve Bank President Susan Collins is scheduled to deliver opening remarks and appear in a televised interview at the Federal Reserve Bank of Boston. 

09:45 AM PMI Composite Flash

The flash Composite Purchasing Managers' Index provides an early estimate of current private sector output by combining information obtained from surveys of around 1,000 manufacturing and service sector companies.

The manufacturing PMI is expected at 52.3 for the November flash versus 52.5 in the October final. Services is seen at 54.8 versus 54.8 in the October final. 10:00 AM Consumer Sentiment 

The University of Michigan's Consumer Survey Center questions households each month on their assessment of current conditions and expectations of future conditions.

The index is expected to be at 50.5 in the November final, from 50.3 in the prior.

10:00 AM Wholesale Inventories (Preliminary)

Wholesale trade measures the dollar value of sales made and inventories held by merchant wholesalers. It is a component of business sales and inventories.

Forecasters expect inventories to decrease by 0.2% m-o-m.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.
